TEAMING AGREEMENT — Template (Prime + Subcontractor for a specific federal procurement)

This is a fill-in-the-blank teaming agreement template between a PRIME (e.g. a Tribally-owned 8(a) program participant) and a SUBCONTRACTOR partner, for the preparation and submittal of a proposal and post-proposal activities on a named procurement (Customer, Solicitation Number, Contracting Office).

Key provisions:

1–3. Identifies the Parties (PRIME and SUBCONTRACTOR), states that by combining capabilities they offer the best opportunity to achieve award and optimum performance, and sets the procurement this Agreement governs (Customer, address, solicitation number).

4. Responsibilities: PRIME prepares and submits the proposal. SUBCONTRACTOR is a strong partner with expertise/experience and relevant past-performance ratings. SUBCONTRACTOR is bound by the prices, terms, conditions, and commitments in its cost proposal and its management/technical areas of responsibility in PRIME's proposal.

5. Proposal tasks: SUBCONTRACTOR provides technical support, corporate experience, key personnel qualifications, and technical/management/cost inputs (per Exhibit A — Statement of Work); supports written communications/presentations; supports the marketing effort with PRIME as team leader. Each Party bears its own proposal/negotiation costs. SUBCONTRACTOR is identified in the proposal as a team member.

6. Award follow-through: If PRIME wins, PRIME agrees to offer SUBCONTRACTOR a subcontract for the covered work, negotiated in good faith, consistent with prime-contract flowdowns, schedules, and required clauses.

7. Term/expiration triggers: decision not to bid; execution of a subcontract; customer decision not to award PRIME; customer finds SUBCONTRACTOR unacceptable; cancellation of the requirement; ineligibility (conflict of interest, suspension, debarment, bankruptcy); uncured material breach (10 business days); or expiration one (1) year from the effective date (extended if the proposal is still under consideration). Nondisclosure obligations (Exhibit B) survive termination.

8. Customer contacts are coordinated between the Parties; SUBCONTRACTOR supports prime-contract negotiations.

9. Public announcements/publicity about the other Party require prior approval.

10. On-premises rules, safeguarding of classified information, mutual indemnification for bodily injury/property damage from a negligent act/omission.

11. Non-solicitation of each other's involved employees during the term (with exceptions).

12. Exclusivity: neither Party actively participates in competing team efforts or competes independently for the work while this Agreement is in effect.

13. Independent contractors — NOT a joint venture, partnership, or pooling arrangement; employees of one are not agents of the other.

14. No sharing of profits/losses; no liability for the other's proposal/pre-contract costs; no indirect/incidental/special/consequential damages.

15. No assignment without consent (except to a successor acquiring substantially all assets).

16. Proprietary information disclosed only per Exhibit B — Nondisclosure Agreement; government regulations apply for classified info.

17. Inventions: solely-conceived inventions belong to the conceiving Party; jointly-conceived are subject to further agreement (and prime-contract terms).

18. Notices/addresses for PRIME and SUBCONTRACTOR.

19. Governing law: the State where the project is organized.

20. Entire-agreement, amendments in writing, severability.

21. Dispute resolution: managers attempt settlement; otherwise binding arbitration under the AAA Commercial Industry Arbitration Rules; performance continues during disputes; award is final and binding.

22. Both Parties exert reasonable efforts to produce a winning proposal and cause selection of PRIME as prime and SUBCONTRACTOR as subcontractor.

23. Signature blocks; effective date.

EXHIBIT A — SUBCONTRACTOR STATEMENT OF WORK: the scope/tasks SUBCONTRACTOR performs under the resulting subcontract, including task-order issuance procedures, pricing schedules (e.g. AF Form 3052), and flowdown of all prime-contract provisions associated with the subcontracted work.

EXHIBIT B — NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT: defines "Proprietary Information" and its exclusions (already known, publicly available, independently developed, disclosed to the Government with unlimited rights); standard of reasonable care; use limited to evaluation/the Agreement; non-disclosure for two years after expiration/termination; carve-out for legally compelled disclosure with notice; no implied patent/IP licenses.
